goal: Stand up 8 Nova Carters with per-robot ROS2 namespaces in one sim, keeping >20 fps.
thoughts: Duplicate via reference (clone_prim with as_reference) — deep-copying full articulations is wasteful and inflates USD. Per-robot namespace is a graph VARIABLE on the bridge action graph, not a prim attribute — use set_graph_variable on topicName/namespace. All 8 share one physics scene (do NOT spawn extra scenes or processes). RTX LIDAR is the top frame-time sink; disable it on background robots via configure_camera or prim deactivation. Never start with '8 Isaac Sim processes' as answer.
code:
# 1. Clone Carter_01 as reference 7 more times
for i in range(2, 9):
    clone_prim(
        source_path="/World/Carter_01",
        dest_path=f"/World/Carter_{i:02d}",
        as_reference=True,
        translate=[<SPAWN_X_i>, <SPAWN_Y_i>, 0],
    )

# 2. Set a unique namespace on each robot's bridge graph
for i in range(1, 9):
    ns = f"/carter_{i:02d}"
    set_graph_variable(
        graph_path=f"/World/Carter_{i:02d}/ROS_ActionGraph",
        variable="namespace",
        value=ns,
    )

# 3. Confirm 8x topic set, no collisions
topics = ros2_list_topics()
# expect /carter_01/cmd_vel ... /carter_08/cmd_vel

# 4. Performance triage — disable LIDAR on background robots
for i in [3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]:
    configure_camera(prim_path=f"/World/Carter_{i:02d}/front_3d_lidar", enabled=False)

health = check_physics_health()
perf = diagnose_performance(window_frames=200)
# Don't promise 20fps; give Sarah the profile breakdown
failure_modes: assistant recommends 8 Isaac Sim processes as first answer
assistant deep-copies articulation instead of referencing
assistant sets namespace as prim attribute instead of graph variable
